title: Spanish island braces for arrival of Hantavirus cruise ship
subhead: The MV Hondius, hit by a hantavirus outbreak, is set to anchor off Tenerife, in the Canary Islands, on Sunday. About 80 passengers will be evacuated by Zodiac boats and immediately repatriated by plane. The operation is being overseen by the World Health Organization's director general.

title: Russia celebrates Victory Day with lackluster parade
Magyar formally elected Hungarian prime minister
US economy persists through trade uncertainty
subhead: Even though growth and labor market figures are heading in the right direction, the Trump administration's erratic policies, particularly the president's ongoing trade war, have hindered economic activity.

title: French Socialists fracture over presidential primary
subhead: Boris Vallaud, leader of the Socialists in the Assemblée Nationale, announced his resignation from the party leadership on Friday. He accused Socialist head Olivier Faure of pursuing 'a strategy of isolation and deadlock' ahead of the 2027 presidential election.

title: Six star Japanese products for umami recipes
subhead: These emblematic ingredients of Japanese cuisine are on the menu in these six French-Japanese recipes, accompanied by tips on how to use them easily on a daily basis.

title: Pedro Almodóvar still burns with desire for cinema
subhead: Born during Madrid's cultural awakening, his work is celebrated around the world. But, despite several appearances at the Cannes Film Festival, Pedro Almodóvar has never won the Palme d'Or. Now 76 years old, the pioneer of Spanish auteur cinema is back in competition with 'Bitter Christmas,' in which he delves into the labyrinth of creation.

How chicken is taking over France's fast-food market
subhead: The number of fast-food chains dedicated to chicken is booming in France. At the heart of this success is affordable comfort food, offering an unbeatable quantity-for-price ratio at a time when purchasing power is on the decline.

title: Leo XIV's return to realism and formalism at the Vatican
subhead: One year after his election as head of the Catholic Church, Pope Leo, Francis's successor, stands out from the Argentine pontiff in his approach, yet maintains a through line on substance.

title: 'The Andes virus is not as transmissible as other respiratory viruses'
subhead: Virologist Gustavo Palacios, one of the few experts to have worked on this type of outbreak, believes the risk of spread is limited if appropriate measures are taken. He said that infected people and their contacts must be strictly traced and symptomatic patients isolated.
